reason: Enabling FIPS mode requires adding a kernel boot parameter (fips=1) and adjusting the system-wide cryptographic policy. The documentation states that enabling FIPS on an already installed system is not supported and may render the system unbootable or cause incompatibilities. This change is high risk and could lock out access, thus it must be treated as too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: reboot required; kernel boot parameter modification; system-wide cryptographic policy change; potential boot failure; possible incompatibility with existing software; risk of losing console/SSH access
